thread.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
thread.definitionDraft: Model Context Protocol is an open protocol for connecting AI applications to tools, data sources, prompts, and resources through standardized client-server integrations.
thread.whyNow: Agentic products increasingly need reliable tool and data access, and MCP is becoming a common integration surface across clients, servers, SDKs, and examples.
thread.useBoundary: Focus on the protocol's technical design, official implementations, and product integrations. Exclude financial analysis, company earnings, or generic tool-use discussions without protocol context.

sourceRequirements.companyStrategyContextPolicy: Company strategy sources may be kept only as optional company_strategy_context targets and must not count toward topic readiness.

sources.title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
sources.whyRelevant: Tool-use foundation paper demonstrating why a protocolized tool layer matters for LLMs.
sources.evidenceQuote: We introduce Toolformer, a model trained to decide which APIs to call, when to call them, what arguments to pass, and how to best integrate the results.
sources.reviewNotes: URL provided in seed. Key foundational paper for tool-use research.
sources.text:
Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools

Tool-use foundation paper demonstrating why a protocolized tool layer matters for LLMs.

Evidence note: We introduce Toolformer, a model trained to decide which APIs to call, when to call them, what arguments to pass, and how to best integrate the results.

URL provided in seed. Key foundational paper for tool-use research.

edges.evidenceNote: The TypeScript SDK implements the protocol defined in the specification repository.
The Python SDK implements the protocol defined in the specification repository.
Claude Code's MCP integration is a product-level implementation of the general protocol described in the main docs.
Research on LLM tool-use motivates the need for standardized tool access, which MCP provides.
The existence and quality of official SDKs signal ecosystem maturity and adoption potential.
